question_text: A motor transfers 4000 joules of energy in 10 seconds. How long would it take this motor to transfer 8000 joules of energy at the same power?
explanation: First, calculate the power: P = 4000 ÷ 10 = 400 W. Then use P = E ÷ t to find time: 400 = 8000 ÷ t, so t = 8000 ÷ 400 = 20 seconds. Since the power is constant, doubling the energy doubles the time needed.
